leadText: Hypnosis (Japanese: さいみんじゅつ Hypnotism) is a non-damaging Psychic-type move introduced in Generation I.

sections.title: Effect
sections.text: Contents 1 Effect 1.1 Generation I 1.2 Generation II 1.3 Generation III 1.4 Generation IV onward 1.5 Generation VIII 1.5.1 Pokémon Legends: Arceus 1.6 Generation IX 1.6.1 Pokémon Legends: Z-A Effect [ edit | edit source ] Generation I [ edit | edit source ] Hypnosis puts the target to sleep . In the Generation I handheld games only, Hypnosis can affect a target behind a substitute . Hypnosis has an accuracy of 60%. Generation II [ edit | edit source ] In the Generation II core series games, when an in-game opponent outside of the Battle Tower uses Hypnosis, the move has a 25% chance to fail in addition to its normal chance to miss. Generation III [ edit | edit source ] Hypnosis has no effect on Pokémon with the Ability Insomnia or Vital Spirit . Hypnosis can be used as the first move of a Pokémon Contest combination , causing Dream Eater and Nightmare to score double the normal appeal if used in the next turn. Generation IV onward [ edit | edit source ] In Diamond and Pearl and Pokémon Battle Revolution , Hypnosis's accuracy was changed to 70%. In Platinum , it was changed back to 60%. In link battles between Diamond and Pearl vs Platinum, HeartGold and SoulSilver, Hypnosis's accuracy is set to 60% regardless of who chose to become leader. Hypnosis can also be used as part of a Contest Spectacular combination , causing certain moves ( Dream Eater , Hex , Nightmare and Wake-Up Slap ) to give an extra three appeal points if used in the next turn. If powered up by a Psychium Z into Z-Hypnosis, the user's Speed stat is raised by one stage. Generation VIII [ edit | edit source ] Pokémon Legends: Arceus [ edit | edit source ] Hypnosis has an accuracy of 70%. It inflicts drowsiness on the target, lasting five turns. Pokémon Legends: Arceus Category Status PP 20 Agile Strong Power — — — Accuracy 70% 70% 90% Action speed modifier Self 0 0 +5 Target 0 +3 0 Additional effect Turns 5 3 7 ? ? ? ? sections.title: Description
sections.text: Description [ edit | edit source ] Games Description Stad Stad2 A special Psychic -type move. The target is hypnotized into a deep sleep. G S C May put the foe to sleep. R S E Colo XD A hypnotizing move that may induce sleep. FR LG Hypnotic suggestion is used to make the foe fall into a deep sleep. D P Pt HG SS PBR B W B2 W2 X Y OR AS S M US UM P E Sw Sh BD SP The user employs hypnotic suggestion to make the target fall into a deep sleep. LA The user employs hypnotic suggestion to make the target drowsy. S V The user employs hypnotic suggestion to make the target fall asleep. ZA The user employs hypnotic suggestion to make targets drowsy.

sections.title: Description
sections.text: Description [ edit | edit source ] Games Description MD R B Makes the target go to sleep, causing it to be incapable of action. MD T D S Afflicts the targeted enemy with a Sleep status condition. A Pokémon with the Sleep status is incapable of action. B S L てきポケモンを すいみんじょうたいにかえる すいみんじょうたいの ポケモンは こうどうできない Conq. The user employs hypnotic suggestion to make the target fall into a deep sleep. MD GtI It causes the Sleep status condition to an enemy. With the Sleep status, the enemy can't do anything. S MD It makes an enemy go to sleep. It makes the enemy terrified if the enemy's sleeping. But if you're attacked by this move while you're sleeping, you'll become the enemy's puppet.

sections.title: Trivia
sections.text: Trivia [ edit | edit source ] Hypnosis is the only 60 accuracy move, not counting Shadow Panic and Shadow End . Hypnosis is the only move whose accuracy changed between games of the same generation, excluding changes in Pokémon Legends: Arceus .
